topics.title: Solving Linear Equations
topics.subtitle: The foundation of all algebra
topics.intro: A linear equation has one variable raised to the power of 1. Solving it means finding the value that makes the equation true.

concepts.title: 🔑 The Golden Rule
concepts.body: Whatever you do to one side of the equation, you must do to the other side. Add, subtract, multiply or divide — always both sides equally.

concepts.title: 📋 Step-by-Step Method
concepts.body:
Step 1: Move all variable terms to one side.
Step 2: Move all constants to the other side.
Step 3: Divide both sides by the coefficient.
Step 4: Check your answer by substituting back.

concepts.title: 🇿🇦 SA Context Example
concepts.body:
Sipho earns R150 per hour. He wants to earn R1 800. How many hours must he work?
150h = 1800 → h = 12 hours.

keyterms.definition: A letter representing an unknown value, e.g. x or h
The number multiplied by the variable, e.g. 3 in 3x
A fixed number with no variable, e.g. the 8 in x + 8 = 12

topics.formula: ax + b = c → x = (c - b) / a
topics.activity: A taxi from Durban to Pietermaritzburg costs R15 plus R2.50 per km. If the total fare was R90, how many km was the trip? Set up and solve the equation.
topics.examTip: Always show your working line by line. NSC markers award method marks even if your final answer is wrong. Never skip steps.

topics.title: Pythagoras Theorem
topics.subtitle: Right-angled triangles and beyond
topics.intro: The Pythagorean Theorem states that in a right-angled triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of squares of the other two sides.

concepts.title: 🔑 The Formula
concepts.body: a² + b² = c² where c is always the hypotenuse (the longest side, opposite the right angle).

concepts.title: 🇿🇦 Real World Use
concepts.body: A builder in Soweto wants to check if a corner is square. He measures 3m along one wall and 4m along another. The diagonal should be exactly 5m. This is the 3-4-5 rule used daily on SA building sites.

keyterms.definition: The longest side of a right-angled triangle, always opposite the 90° angle
An angle of exactly 90°, shown by a small square in diagrams

topics.formula:
a² + b² = c²
c = √(a² + b²)
a = √(c² - b²)
topics.activity: A ladder leans against a wall. The foot of the ladder is 1.5m from the wall and the ladder reaches 2m up the wall. How long is the ladder? Show full working.
topics.examTip: Always identify the hypotenuse FIRST before substituting. Label your triangle in the diagram — examiners give marks for correct diagrams.
